untranslatable_reason: This signal requires pair/spread analysis across correlated instruments (rolling 126-day median spread, correlation > 0.75, divergence measured in standard deviations of spread). The allowed indicators are single-instrument technical indicators only. There is no support for: (1) multi-instrument correlation calculations, (2) inter-instrument spread calculations, (3) rolling historical median/std dev of spreads, or (4) relative pricing comparisons between instrument pairs. The backtest engine would need custom spread indicators and pair correlation metrics not in the allowed list.
translation_notes: Signal is a statistical arbitrage/pairs trading strategy requiring cross-instrument analysis. The core logic involves: detecting when correlated pairs diverge beyond 1.5 std devs from their 126-day median spread, then taking opposing positions. Exit occurs at 1.0 std dev reversion or 2.5 std dev stop loss. This cannot be expressed using only single-instrument price/momentum indicators.
